layman_headline: Fear grips the market — patience beats FOMO right now.
layman_explanation: The overall crypto market is in a fearful state with Bitcoin slipping back to $63,800 and most altcoins bleeding. None of the coins on the watchlist present a clean, high-conviction swing trade entry today. DEXE surged but is dangerously overbought at RSI 85, BONK is in freefall with no sign of a bottom, and HYPE just dropped 11% in a week with mediocre risk/reward. Ethereum is the most interesting candidate with RSI near oversold and a Bullish Marubozu candle forming, but it's still below all major moving averages with no confirmed reversal yet.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait. Set price alerts on Ethereum around $1,780 — if it dips there and holds support at $1,700 with volume, that's a much better entry. Don't chase anything in this fearful, choppy market.
expert_reasoning: I evaluated all 10 swing candidates and none meet the bar for a high-confidence entry. DEXE has RSI 85.45 — our strongest reliable signal (RSI at 80% win rate) screams correction risk, not entry. BONK RSI 33 is attractive but the chart bias is -1.40 with a Bearish Marubozu — catching a falling knife. HYPE has a bullish chart (score +1.10) but RSI 59 is mildly overbought and a -10.8% weekly drop in a fear market suggests further downside. ETH at RSI 35 is the most compelling setup — approaching oversold on the second-largest crypto, with a Bullish Marubozu candle and +6.5% 5-day momentum suggesting early accumulation. However, price remains below all MAs (bearish structure), the chart bias is neutral (0.0), and BTC dominance at 56.3% is crushing alts. I need to see ETH reclaim $1,800 resistance with volume confirmation before committing capital. The Iran strikes headline from CoinTelegraph adds geopolitical risk that could push BTC below $62K and drag everything lower.
risk_factors: Iran-related geopolitical escalation could trigger a broader risk-off selloff pushing BTC below $62K
BTC dominance at 56.3% continues to suffocate altcoin performance — no alt season signal
Fear & Greed at 27 is improving but still in Fear territory — sentiment can deteriorate quickly
watch_list: ETH price action around $1,800 resistance — a close above with volume confirms the reversal
Bitcoin holding $62,500 support — a break below invalidates all alt setups
Fear & Greed index crossing above 30 — would signal sentiment shift supporting new entries
market_context: The crypto market is in a fearful, risk-off phase with BTC at $63,800 and dominance at 56.3% crushing altcoins. Geopolitical tensions from Iran strikes are adding pressure alongside an AI-related narrative shift away from crypto.
context_banner: Market fear and geopolitical headwinds demand patience — the best swing entries emerge when clarity returns, not when chaos reigns.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 35 approaching oversold territory — our most reliable signal (80% win rate) flags a potential bounce setup, but needs confirmation above resistance.
Bitcoin sagging under $62.5K as Iran strikes add pressure — geopolitical risk is a headwind for any new long positions (CoinTelegraph 67% reliable).
Fear & Greed at 27 (Fear) improving from 25 — trend is positive but not yet at levels where contrarian buys have strong edges historically.
ETH derivatives reset article signals institutional positioning may be shifting — watch for follow-through in spot markets.
DEXE and HYPE both score +1.10 chart bias but DEXE's RSI 85 makes it a trap, and HYPE's 1:1 risk/reward at tight support doesn't justify entry.
